questions.question: What will be the output of the following code: interface Animal { void sound(); } class Dog implements Animal { public void sound() { System.out.println("Woof"); } } interface Animal { default void sound() { System.out.println("Animal sound"); } }
A class implements an interface with a method which is declared as a default method in the interface. Does the class override the default method? Explain.
If a method in an interface is declared as 'static', can it be overridden by a class that implements the interface? Explain the reason behind this.
